description: The DeCavalcante crime family is an Italian-American Mafia crime family operating primarily in North Jersey, particularly in Elizabeth, Newark, Linden, and West New York. Founded around the 1900s by Phil Amari, it is considered by some to be the 'Sixth Family' due to its strong ties with New York's Five Families. The family engages in racketeering, gambling, drug trafficking, extortion, and other illicit activities. It maintains alliances with several other Mafia families, including the Gambino and Philadelphia families.

country_links.evidence_quote: The family also has links to Ribera in Sicily , where many of the organization's members have their origins.
country_links.context: The organization has links to Ribera in Sicily, where many of its members have their origins.
